Seq2Seq: Information Bottleneck
The entire source sequence is compressed into
one fixed context vector
The decoder cannot directly revisit earlier encoder states
This becomes more problematic for
longer sequences
This bottleneck motivates attention.

Why did Transformers replace recurrence?

Self-attention ‚Üí global access, parallelizable.

Attention: The Decoder Looks Back at the Whole Input
Seq2Seq compresses the whole input into one vector.
Attention removes this restriction.
At each decoding step, the decoder can use all encoder states.
12
Attention lets the decoder dynamically choose where to look in the input.

Self-Attention: Basic Idea
Models relationships among tokens in the same sequence
Each token attends to other tokens in that sequence
Produces a contextualized representation for each token

Self-Attention: From Scores to Weights
Query
(Q): what the current token is looking for
Key
(K): how each token can be matched
Value
(V): the information contributed by each token

Step 1: Compute similarity scores between the query and all keys
Step 2: Apply
softmax
to convert the scores into attention weights
Step 3: Take the weighted sum of the values

Dot Product is a Similarity Score
If two vectors are identical ‚Üí Cosine similarity = 1.0
If two vectors are unrelated (perpendicular) ‚Üí Cosine similarity = 0.0
If two vectors are opposite ‚Üí Cosine similarity = -1.0

possible_slide_title: Self-Attention: Roles of Query, Key, and Value
visible_text:
Term
Meaning
Role in Self-Attention
Query (Q)
The search request
What information does this token need?
Key (K)
The available knowledge
How important is this token to others?
Value (V)
The actual content
What information is passed forward?
Physical Interpretation: Self-attention is like searching a knowledge base.
Query (Q): what information this token needs
Key (K): how this token can be matched
Value (V): the information this token contributes

Sinusoidal Positional Encoding: Basic Idea
Each token position is represented by a deterministic vector built from sine and cosine functions at different frequencies.
37
https://arxiv.org/abs/1706.03762
‚Ä¶‚Ä¶

Sinusoidal Positional Encoding: Results
Each token position is represented by a deterministic vector built from sine and cosine functions at different frequencies.
38
Low-frequency dimensions capture coarse position; high-frequency dimensions capture fine position.

Intuition for Sinusoidal Positional Encoding
Different dimensions use sine and cosine waves with different frequencies, so each position gets a distinct pattern.
Nearby positions have similar encodings
Different frequencies capture both local and global position patterns
The encoding extends naturally to unseen positions
Sinusoidal encoding gives each position a unique, multi-scale signature.
39

Sinusoidal Positional Encoding: Visualization
How Sinusoidal Encoding Represents Relative Position
Nearby positions have similar encodings
Different frequencies capture both small and large offsets
Relative distance is reflected in how the encoding changes across positions
Attention can use these patterns to reason about token order
Relative position is reflected by how the sinusoidal pattern changes from one token to the next.

Multi-Head Attention: Multiple Views of the Same Sequence

Attention
: relates tokens in one sequence to tokens in another sequence
Self-attention
: relates tokens within the same sequence
In the original Transformer, the encoder and decoder use self-attention, and the decoder also uses encoder‚Äďdecoder attention
Attention vs. Self-Attention

Cross-Attention: Another Example with Dimensions
Queries (Q) come from one sequence
Keys (K) and Values (V) come from another sequence
The output is a weighted combination of the values, based on query‚Äďkey similarity
Cross-attention lets one sequence retrieve relevant information from another sequence.

Multi-Head Attention: Why Use Multiple Heads?
Head 1
Head 2
Head 3
A single attention head captures only one type of relationship at a time
Multiple heads allow the model to attend to different patterns in parallel
Different heads may focus on different tokens or different types of dependencies
Multiple heads provide multiple views of the same sequence.

Multi-Head Attention: Concatenate and Project
Each head works in a lower-dimensional subspace.
45

Transformer: Summary
The Transformer models a sequence by combining content, context, and position.
